records.m_pText: Her First Bow
Is this what they call a bow?
That’s right.$nFirst time using one, [PCF1]?
Yes. I have heard about them,$nbut never had a chance to$nactually lay hands upon one.
Would it be alright if I gave it$na try over there?
Equipping weapons suited to$nyour job is very important.
Mm-hm, it has a major effect on$nyour attack power, too.
The knight’s weapon of choice is the$nsword! The white mage’s, a staff!$nAnd the black mage’s, a rod!
Ah! [PCF1] is back!$nWhat did you think of the bow?
Well…$nI think, perhaps, I expected too much.
It seems to me I could do better$nwith my bare hands.
That doesn’t make any sense.$nThe bow is a powerful weapon,$nafter all.
I know! Show her by loosing an$narrow at that fence over there.
Alright, here goes!
……! Bull’s-eye!
What was that stick-like object$nthat flew from the bow?
And what is an arrow…?
Eh?$nYou’re kidding…right?
Overpriced Love
[PCM2], where’s that girl$nyou’ve been seeing?$nShe hasn’t come around lately.
Did she finally get fed up with$nyou and leave?
I’d had enough of her, anyway.
Did she offer you a reason?
Well, she’d been saying how she$nwanted this gold lamé handbag,$nso I finally bought it for her.
You actually ended up buying it…
That had to be rather pricey.$nI’m surprised you could afford it.
She seemed really happy when she$nwalked around with her new handbag.
But soon, all I could see was the$nhandbag. It looked to me like the bag$nwas walking around with her.
It was one huge wet blanket on$nour relationship.
Ha ha! That’s what happens when$nyou don’t live within your means!
You’re telling me.$nI’m almost broke now.
That bad, huh?
It is. And tonight we’ll be$nroughing it outdoors!
Right, [PCM1]?
Wait, whaaat!?
[PCM2]’s Worry
I am so glad to see you are safe,$n[PCF2].
Ah ha ha, sorry to have worried$nyou like that!
What I can’t figure out is why all$nthose women would fall for that$nman’s tricks.
Indeed. I would have refused that$nold degenerate out of hand!
Young degenerates aren’t much better.$n[PCM2] wouldn’t stop going on$nabout “technique”…
Oh, but it was [PCM2] who$nwas the most concerned for you.
And it was [PCM2] who was$nthe most flustered when you$ndisappeared, [PCF2].
And he only got that angry at$nthe red mage because of you,$nyou know.
I guess you’re right. I suppose$nhe’s “white” today, then.
Why? Does he have days when$nhe’s “black”?
Well… Say, [PCF1].$nWhat do you think$nof [PCM2]?
What do I think of him?
Oh, no! I didn’t mean it like that!
Mean it like what?
Umm…uh… What I mean is…he’s$nmuttonheaded, lecherous, and$nlacking all social graces, but$nevery once in a while—
Did somebody call my name?
Or were you just gossiping about me?$nRemember, I’m always there for$nyou—even when you don’t want me.
Eeew! Creepy! And no, we didn’t$nmention your name! Now go away!
Organizing Items
Now that we have the$nsalve-maker asterisk…
It should let us heighten the$nperformance of our items.
And combine items to make them$nmore powerful as well.
Hmm, that sounds handy.$nBut not really very exciting, is it?
I know, right?$nFar too humdrum for us.
In other words, you two are too$nlazy to organize your items!
No, we’re not lazy!$nThere are simply other$nthings we’d rather do.
It’s all fine and dandy to have$nacquired the salve-maker job, but$nyour item bags are a jumbled mess.
It must take forever to find$nwhat you need when you need it!
For example, [PCF2],$ndon’t keep your potions$nwith your hi-potions!
What? They’re both restoratives,$nso why not?
And you, [PCM2]!$nDon’t lump antidotes, echo herbs,$nand wakeup bells together!
Why not? Together they’re like a$ncure-all!
What if you only need one of them?$nYou’ll never find it in time! You$nshould learn from [PCF1]’s$norganizational skills.
Ah, but [PCF1], there’s no$nneed to keep each of your pills$nseparately.
Oh, yes, of course!
[PCM1], you and [PCF1]$nare sure sticklers for detail.
[PCF2] and I,$non the other hand…$nWell, to each his own, right?
That’s right!$nDon’t sweat the details is my…my…$nAaah…aaah…
Nooo!$n[PCF2], sneeze the other—!
AAAH-CHOOO!!!
Way… You just sneezed away$nall the powder items.
Well, that means fewer items to$norganize, right? Let’s go.
*sigh*
Lord DeRosso’s Epic Oratory
[PCM2], you were sawing$nlogs almost the entire time$nLord DeRosso was speaking.
Sawing logs?
She means you were sleeping.
Ah, so it was that obvious?
Obvious? You were snoring$nlike a bear!
I was!? You’ll have to forgive me.$nEven I know that’s really rude.
That it is. Even if that was an$nincredibly long bit of oratory.
That’s right, [PCM2]!$nYou were really rude!
I mean, your snoring even woke$nme up!
What!? You mean you were$nsleeping, too, [PCF2]!?
The Four Shieldbearer Warriors
Say, Zatz, I met Steiner over at$nCentral Command.
So that wily fox yet lives, eh?$nHe was always the brains of the$nFour Warriors of the Shieldbearers.
How’s he doing?
Good. He’s been moving up in the$nworld while we’ve been hiding$nout here.
He was clad in some magnificent armor.$nIt was really shiny—and by the look$non his face, really uncomfortable.
And Neuer? Is she still trying to$nsteal Commander Goodman away$nfrom his wife?
Didn’t see Neuer, so who knows?$nBut rumor has it she’s battling$nSteiner for a promotion.
Hmm, they’re fighting to get ahead$nof one another? That doesn’t sound$nlike the Four Warriors we know.
I’d wager scrambling up that$nladder of success is a lot more$ntrouble than we could imagine.
Yeah, don’t think I’ll ever$nunderstand the allure. Just$norder me into the fray to bust$nsome heads. That’s all I need.
Bah ha ha! Well said!$nWell said, indeed!
